Weekly Diet Plan: Hey Arjun,

Great to have you on board! I've built this plan around your existing eating pattern—breakfast at 8:30, lunch at 1:30, evening tea at 6:30, and dinner at 9—so it fits naturally into your office routine. Your goal is solid: 74 kg in 12 weeks means we're looking at steady, sustainable fat loss while keeping your strength up in the gym. I've kept your favorite foods (dal, paneer, idli, curd rice) front and center, and thrown in some variety to stop things getting boring. The weekend eating thing? We'll tackle that as we go, but the foundation here is consistent, affordable meals that don't feel like punishment. Let's go.

---

**MONDAY**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
1 cup poha [approx 150g] made with 1 tbsp oil, 1 small onion, 1 green chilli, and a pinch of turmeric. Serve with 1 small lime wedge on the side.
(P: 4g | C: 38g | F: 8g | ~210 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1.5 katoris cooked rice [approx 225g], 1 katori dal [approx 150g cooked], 1 small bowl sabzi (any seasonal veg—carrot, beans, peas, cooked with minimal oil), and a small bowl curd [approx 100g].
(P: 18g | C: 62g | F: 5g | ~390 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
1 cup tea with 1 tbsp roasted chana [approx 30g] and 2-3 small biscuits or 1 small rusk.
(P: 6g | C: 20g | F: 3g | ~120 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis [approx 60g], 100g paneer bhurji or paneer curry cooked with 1 tbsp oil and onion, 1 small bowl salad (tomato, cucumber, lettuce), and 1 tbsp curd on the side.
(P: 22g | C: 28g | F: 14g | ~330 kcal)

---

**TUESDAY**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
2 idlis [approx 120g] with 1 small bowl sambar [approx 150g] and 1 tbsp coconut chutney [approx 20g].
(P: 6g | C: 34g | F: 4g | ~190 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1.5 katoris cooked rice [approx 225g], 1 katori dal [approx 150g], 1 small bowl stir-fried sabzi (broccoli, cabbage, or green beans with 1 tsp oil), and 1 small bowl curd.
(P: 18g | C: 62g | F: 4g | ~380 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
1 cup tea with 1 tbsp roasted chana [approx 30g] and 1 small slice of toast [approx 30g] with a thin spread of peanut butter.
(P: 7g | C: 19g | F: 5g | ~135 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 1 katori paneer and pea curry [approx 150g paneer + peas cooked with 1 tbsp oil], salad, and 1 tbsp curd.
(P: 24g | C: 28g | F: 13g | ~330 kcal)

---

**WEDNESDAY**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
1 cup poha [approx 150g] with 1 tbsp oil, onion, and green chilli, plus 1 boiled egg on the side.
(P: 10g | C: 38g | F: 12g | ~310 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1.5 katoris rice, 1 katori dal, 1 small bowl sabzi (any veg), curd.
(P: 18g | C: 62g | F: 5g | ~390 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
1 cup tea with 1 tbsp roasted chana and 2 small biscuits.
(P: 6g | C: 20g | F: 3g | ~120 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 100g paneer tikka or paneer fry cooked with minimal oil and spices, salad, curd.
(P: 22g | C: 28g | F: 11g | ~310 kcal)

---

**THURSDAY**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
2 idlis with sambar and coconut chutney.
(P: 6g | C: 34g | F: 4g | ~190 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1.5 katoris rice, 1 katori dal, 1 small bowl sabzi, curd. (Same as Tuesday—no need to overthink it.)
(P: 18g | C: 62g | F: 4g | ~380 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
1 cup tea with 1 tbsp roasted chana and 1 small slice toast with peanut butter.
(P: 7g | C: 19g | F: 5g | ~135 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 1 katori paneer and pea curry, salad, curd.
(P: 24g | C: 28g | F: 13g | ~330 kcal)

---

**FRIDAY**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
1 cup poha with oil, onion, chilli, and 1 boiled egg.
(P: 10g | C: 38g | F: 12g | ~310 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1.5 katoris rice, 1 katori dal, 1 small bowl sabzi, curd.
(P: 18g | C: 62g | F: 5g | ~390 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
1 cup tea with 1 tbsp roasted chana and 2 biscuits.
(P: 6g | C: 20g | F: 3g | ~120 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 100g paneer bhurji or paneer curry, salad, curd.
(P: 22g | C: 28g | F: 14g | ~330 kcal)

---

**SATURDAY**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
2 idlis with sambar and coconut chutney.
(P: 6g | C: 34g | F: 4g | ~190 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1.5 katoris rice, 1 katori dal, 1 small bowl sabzi, curd.
(P: 18g | C: 62g | F: 5g | ~390 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
Here's where we keep it steady on the weekend: 1 cup tea with 1 tbsp roasted chana and 1 small slice toast with peanut butter. If you're tempted to grab extra snacks, have a glass of water first and a small bowl of curd—fills you up, keeps you on track.
(P: 7g | C: 19g | F: 5g | ~135 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 100g paneer tikka or paneer fry, salad, curd.
(P: 22g | C: 28g | F: 11g | ~310 kcal)

---

**SUNDAY**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
1 cup poha with oil, onion, chilli, and 1 boiled egg.
(P: 10g | C: 38g | F: 12g | ~310 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1.5 katoris rice, 1 katori dal, 1 small bowl sabzi, curd.
(P: 18g | C: 62g | F: 5g | ~390 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
1 cup tea with 1 tbsp roasted chana and 2 biscuits.
(P: 6g | C: 20g | F: 3g | ~120 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 1 katori paneer and pea curry, salad, curd.
(P: 24g | C: 28g | F: 13g | ~330 kcal)

---

**Quick Notes:**

• Your dal is your best friend—it's cheap, filling, and packed with protein. Don't skip it.
• When you cook sabzi, use just 1 tsp oil per serving and add a pinch of cumin or turmeric for flavor without extra calories.
• Curd at lunch and dinner keeps you full and adds protein. Buy the plain stuff, not sweetened yogurt.
• On your gym days (evenings), have your lunch and snack as usual, then hit your workout. You don't need anything fancy post-workout—your dinner 2-3 hours later will cover recovery.
• Roasted chana is your go-to snack because it's affordable, portable, and keeps you satisfied. Buy it in bulk.
• Water: you're already at 2L—keep it up. Add a pinch of lemon if it helps you drink more.

You've got this. Stick to the plan, trust the process, and let's check in next week.

Cheers,
Your Coach
